post.description:
Make a payment using credit card, cash, cheque, profile, token, Apple Pay, or Google Pay. Each payment type has its own json definition passed in the
body. For all payments you have the standard Billing, Shipping, Comments, etc. fields that are optional. Only the amount is required along with the
payment data for card, cash, cheque, profile, and token. You must change the payment_method for each payment type. Credit Card - "card",
Payment Profile - "payment_profile", Single Use Token - "token", Cash - "cash", Cheque - "cheque", Apple Pay - "apple_pay",
Google Pay - "google_pay", 3D Secure token - "3d_secure_token".

200.description: Payment response containing the payment details as well as if the payment was approved or declined.
Payment response containing the payment details as well as if the transaction was approved or declined.

post.description: Void a transaction. You can void payments, returns, and pre-auths completions. It will cancel that transaction.

200.description: Payment response containing the payment details as well as if the transaction was approved or declined.

post.description: Complete a pre-authorized payment. The amount of the transaction to complete must be less than or equal to the original pre-auth amount. Complete must be set to true.

200.description: Payment response containing the payment details as well as if the transaction was approved or declined.

parameters.name: merchant_data
parameters.description: The merchant_data attribute value returned from the initiating Payments API request. The value represents a unique payment ID.

200.description: Payment response containing the payment details as well as if the transaction was approved or declined.

post.description: Query for transactions using a date range and optional search criteria. This method allows you to page your search results if you are expecting a lot of results to be returned. The page start value begins at 1 and the results will be limited to 500 rows. If no records are found the API will return a 200 response message with an empty array. For details on the parameters and allowed values for Criteria please [visit the documentation](https://docs.na.worldline-solutions.com/build-your-integration/payment-apis/payment-sdks/analyze-payments)

get.description: This report provides information about a merchant and their sub-merchants.
This report provides information about a merchant's specific sub-merchant.

This report provides credit card settlement and fee information. It is helpful to understand the credit date, amount, and fees of each settlement. You can retrieve settlement data with up to a three-month range.

Note:

* Our system calculates settlement using Pacific Time (PT) with midnight cut off.

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.description: The start date (inclusive) '2020-04-21'. Must be on or before end_date.

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.description: The end date (inclusive) '2020-05-30'. Must be no more than 3 months passed the start_date and cannot include today's date.

get.description:
This report provides details on fees applied to your monthly statement. For partners we also provide comission and fees charged to your sub merchants.

Note:

* Our system calculates fees using Pacific Time (PT) with midnight cut off.
This report provides details on fees applied to your FlexCommissions payouts.

parameters.name: report_days
parameters.description: Number of days from today to generate the report for

parameters.name: filter_value
parameters.description: Filer for payments with state - Scheduled, On Hold, Superseded, In Process, Approved, Declined, Pending Payment, Full Payment

parameters.name: start_row
parameters.description: The first row number in the result set.

parameters.name: end_row
parameters.description: The last row number in the result set.

post.description: Create a new Payment Profile using either a card, a single-use token, a transaction id, or bank credentials. You must supply one of them. You may store a card and bank credentials on the same profile.

get.description: Get a Payment Profile using the profile ID, also known as the Customer Code.

put.description:
Update an existing Payment Profile. For a single card profile supplying token or
card data will work. For multi-card profiles you have to update the card data with
the update and delete card endpoints.

delete.description: Delete a Payment Profile using the profile ID, also known as the Customer Code.

get.description: Get all of the cards on the profile.

post.description: Add a card to the profile. Note that there is a default limit of 1 card per profile. You can change this in your Profiles settings in the back office.

requestBody.description: The card information that will be added to the profile.

get.description: Get the details of a card on the profile.

put.description: Update the details of a card on the profile. A card number can only be updated when an account is configured to only allow 1 card per profile.

requestBody.description: The card information that will be updated on the profile.

delete.description: Delete a card on the profile.

get.description: Get a Recurring Payment Profile using account ID, also known as ID.

post.description: NOTE that the full URL for this API call is https://api.na.bambora.com/scripts/tokenization/tokens. Turn a credit card into a token using this service. This helps lessen your PCI scope by not passing the credit card information to your server. Instead you turn the card number into a token in the client app, then send the token to the server. When you send the token to Bambora to make a payment, Bambora then looks up the card number from that token and makes the payment. Tokens can also be used to create payment profiles.

200.description: Token response object. One will always be returned even if the data or card is invalid. The validity of the card is not checked with this API call.

amount.description: A decimal value in dollars. Uses up to two decimal places. Max value is account specific. Default max value is 1000.

ship_same_as_ord.description: If true, the provided Billing Address information will also be used as the Shipping Address information.

device_channel.description: Type of channel used to initiate the transaction. 02 = Browser, 03 = 3DS Requestor.

customer_ip.description: IP Address of the api consumer. Required if calculating risk score.

term_url.description: Callback URL used with 3D Secure payment processing.

recurring_payment.description:
A recurring transaction is a transaction where a cardholder had provided permission to a merchant to
periodically charge his/her account number for recurring goods or services. The recurring payment
indicator may be set for credit card based pre-auth, capture and purchase transactions.

cvd.description: Security code on the back of the credit card. This can be set to mandatory in the back office.

visa_checkout_call_id.description: The Visa Checkout payment request ID.

complete.description: set to false for Pre-Authorize, and true to complete a payment

passthrough.description: False or null indicates a regular (Worldline-decrypted) Apple Pay transaction. True indicates a passthrough (externally-decrypted) transaction.

apple_pay_merchant_id.description: The Apple Pay merchant ID. Not required for passthrough transactions.

payment_token.description: The encrypted payment token provided by Apple Pay, which you then base64-encode. For passthrough, decrypt the token before encoding it.

complete.description: set to false for Pre-Authorize, and true to complete a payment

ApplePay.description:
Apple Pay Only - required when attempting to process apple_pay transactions.
Note that the 'card' object returned in a successful response will have a 'last_four' property value that is based on the source Apple Pay Wallet and its Device Account Number.

transaction_payload.description: Payload that contains the encrypted Google payment token (required to complete a transaction), along with other security information.

complete.description: set to false for Pre-Authorize, and true to complete a payment

GooglePay.description: Google Pay Only - required when attempting to process google_pay transactions.

payment_token.description: The payment token you retrieved from the service.

ProcessorTokenRequest.description: Payment with Processor Token. The payment_method must be 'processor_token'.

source.description: This will contain the name of the processor that is hosting the tokenization feature.

ProcessorTokenResponse.description: Token information generated from card request, or what was used to submit payment.

complete.description: Set to false for Pre-Authorize, and true to complete a payment

card_id.description: Which credit card to use. Starts at 1 for the first card. You must configure how many cards can be stored by visiting the profile options in the back office.

ProfilePurchase.description: Payment with a Payment Profile. The payment_method must be 'payment_profile'

complete.description: Set to false for Pre-Authorize, and true to complete a payment

code.description: the payment token you retrieved from the service.

visa_checkout_call_id.description: The Visa Checkout payment request ID.

TokenPurchase.description: Payment with a single-use token. The payment_method must be 'token'

complete.description: Set to false for Pre-Authorize, and true to complete a payment

auth_required.description: If set to true the transaction will not continue processing unless 3DS authentication is successful.

disable_method_url.description: Indicates whether or not BIC Method URL has been disabled for the current transaction.

threeDS_server_transaction_id.description: The threeDS\_server\_transaction\_id returned by the call to the PrepInfo endpoint.

3d_secure_token.description: Payment with a 3D Secure Session Data token.  Reference a previously processed 3D Secure transaction processed through the 3D Secure 2.0 API.  This will apply the associated card number, card expiry, and 3D Secure results to your payment request. The payment_method must be '3d_secure_token'

address_line2.description: Additional address information(eg; suite number, apartment number, etc)

province.description: 2 characters. Set to -- for any countries except for US and Canada. Impacts risk score.

country.description: Valid ISO 3166-1 country code. Impacts risk score.

phone_country_code.description: The country code of the phone number provided.

phone_type.description: The phone type of the phone number provided - m (Mobile), h (Home) or w (Work).

address_line2.description: Additional address information(eg; suite number, apartment number, etc)

phone_country_code.description: The country code of the phone number provided.

address_line2.description: Additional address information(eg; suite number, apartment number, etc)

phone_country_code.description: The country code of the phone number provided.

phone_type.description: The phone type of the phone number provided - m (Mobile), h (Home) or w (Work).

authorizing_merchant_id.description: The id of the merchant that authorized the transaction.

approved.description: Approval status of payment transaction. 0 if the transaction is not approved. 1 if the transaction is approved.

message.description: Message containing information about the transactions status

created.description: Time stamp of when the transaction occurred

risk_score.description: The risk score represents the percentage of probability that the transaction is fraudulent. For example, a score of 10 represents a 10% possibility that the transaction is fraudulent.

amount.description: A decimal value in dollars. Uses up to two decimal places.

merchant_data.description: Returned only for 3D Secure payment requests. This value can be used as the {id} value when creating your 'continue' endpoint URL.

contents.description: Returned only for 3D Secure payment requests. This should be embedded in the user's browser client and this needs to be displayed to the customer to redirect them to the 3D Secure processing page.

card_type.description: The type of card used in the transaction. AM = American Express, DI = Diners, JB = JCB, MC = MasterCard, NN = Discover, VI = Visa, PV = Visa Debit, MD = Debit Mastercard

last_four.description: For all payments completed using a credit card, this field will contain the last four digits of the card number used. Payments completed using ApplePay will return last four digits of the Device Primary Account Number (DPAN) associated to the transaction.

card_bin.description: For all payments completed using a credit card, this field will contain the Bank Identification Number (BIN) of the card used. The field will be set to either the first six or first eight digits of the card PAN. Payments completed using ApplePay will return the first six digits of the Device Primary Account Number (DPAN) associated to the transaction. This field will not be returned for credit card transactions processed using a Processor Token.

eci.description: The electronic commerce indicator (ECI) returned back in the transaction response. NOTE: No longer returned for 3DS 2.0, please see status_id instead.

address_match.description: Result of Address verification. 0 if Adddress Verification Service(AVS) is disabled or AVS fails. 1 if AVS is enabled and address and postal/zip code match

postal_result.description: Result of postal verification. 0 if the postal\_code parameter does does not match the customers address records or if AVS was not processed for the transaction. 1 if the postal\_code parameter matches the consumers address records at the issuing bank.

status_id.description: Status/result of a 3DS 2 transaction.  Set to a value of 1-4, see status for a text description.

status.description: Status/result of a 3DS 2 transaction. 1 = Authenticated, 2 = Attempted, 3 = Non participating, 4 = Failed

error_id.description: Returned if status is 'Failed'.  Set to a value of 1-3, see error for a text description.

error.description: Returned if status is 'Failed'.  1 = 3D Secure service is not enabled, 2 = Service temporarily unavailble, 3 = Internal error

id.description: A unique avs response code. For a description of each code see https://docs.na.worldline-solutions.com/build-your-integration/security-and-fraud-APIs/address-verification-system-avs.

message.description: Message containing details of the avs status. Corresponding to each avs\_id, there is an avs_message describing the status of address verification service. For example If avs\_id = 5 then avs\_message = Invalid AVS Response.

processed.description: Is true if the issuing bank has successfully processed an AVS check on the transaction. Is false if no AVS check was performed.

card_type.description: The type of card used in the transaction. AM = American Express, DI = Diners, JB = JCB, MC = MasterCard, NN = Discover, VI = Visa, PV = Visa Debit, MD = Debit Mastercard

last_four.description: The last 4 digits of the credit card used in the transaction

address_match.description: Result of Address verification. 0 if Adddress Verification Service(AVS) is disabled or AVS fails. 1 if AVS is enabled and address and postal/zip code match.

avs_result.description: Result of street address and Postal/Zip match/mismatch. 0 in case of mismatch. 1 in case of match.

expiry_month.description: a 2 digit representation of the expiry month. For example March is 03.

expiry_year.description: a 2 digit representation of the expiry year. For example 2016 is 16.

href.description: Url to perform the void, return, completion etc.

method.description: Http method. post, put, get etc.

amount.description: A decimal value in dollars. Must be less than or equal to the original purchase amount.
A decimal value in dollars. Must be equal to the original purchase amount. You can void purchases as well as pre-auths and returns.

authorizing_merchant_id.description: The id of the merchant that authorized the transaction.

created.description: Time stamp of when the transaction occurred

device_channel.description: Type of channel used to initiate the transaction. 02 = Browser, 03 = 3DS Requestor.

amount.description: A decimal value in dollars. Uses up to two decimal places.
A decimal value in dollars. Uses up to two decimal places.

complete.description: False for Pre-Authorize, and true for a payment complete.

card_id.description: card_id is always 0 and is deprecated so should be ignored.

merchant_id.description: The id of the merchant for this settlement.

currency.description: The currency abbreviation for this settlement.

settlement_net_amount.description: Shows the total net amount of the settlement.

settlement_date.description: The day (PT) the funds are expected to be processed by the bank and settled. If there is no date scheduled yet then it will return "0001-01-01"

sale_amount_total.description: Total amount of sales in dollars and cents

returned_amount_total.description: Total amount of returns in dollars and cents

chargebacks_amount_total.description: Total amount of chargebacks in dollars and cents

discount_rate_fee_total.description: Total of the per-transaction fees that is charged based upon the total amount of the transaction size.

chargeback_fee_total.description: Chargeback fees are applied against transactions that were contested by a credit card owner.

gst_tax_fee_total.description: The total GST applied to transaction fees. GST is only added to chargeback fees.

reserves_held.description: The amount currently held in a Bambora reserve account.

reserves_released.description: Amount released from the reserve account during the current statement period.

reserves_forward.description: Total amount that Bambora held in a reserve account as of the previous statement period.

billing_period.description: The billing period that fees and commissions have been applied to.  Billing periods are monthly where this field will always be set as the 1st day of the month.

parent_id.description: The id of the parent merchant for this statement. This field is only returned for channel partner accounts.

parent_name.description: The name of the parent merchant for this statement. This field is only returned for channel partner accounts.

statement_id.description: The id of the statement for the associated billing period.

merchant_id.description: The id of the merchant for this statement.

merchant_name.description: The name of the merchant for this statement. This field is only returned for channel partner accounts.

fee_type.description: The type of fee being charged.

fee_volume.description: The count of the associated fee being charged.

fee_rate.description: The rate of the fee being charged.

commission_rate.description: The commission rate to be collected against the fee. This field is only returned for channel partner accounts.

commission_amount.description: The commission amount collected against the fee. This field is only returned for channel partner accounts.

payout_id.description: The id of the commission payout.

amount.description: The amount of the commission payout.

start_datetime.description: The start date for the report.

end_datetime.description: The end date for the report.

expected_settlement_date.description: The expected date the payout will reach the Partner.

statement_id.description: The statement that the commission payout will appear on.

status.description: The status of the commission payout.

mastercard_enabled.description: Whether the merchant is configured to process MasterCard.

visa_enabled.description: Whether the merchant is configured to process Visa.

amex_enabled.description: Whether the merchant is configured to process Amex.

discover_enabled.description: Whether the merchant is configured to process Discover.

diners_enabled.description: Whether the merchant is configured to process Diners.

jcb_enabled.description: Whether the merchant is configured to process JCB.

mastercard_debit_enabled.description: Whether the merchant is configured to process MasterCard Debit.

visa_debit_enabled.description: Whether the merchant is configured to process Visa Debit.

checkout_enabled.description: Whether the merchant is configured to use Checkout.

payment_profile_enabled.description: Whether the merchant is configured to use Payment Profiles.

recurring_billing_enabled.description: Whether the merchant is configured to use Recurring Billing.

credit_card_batch_enabled.description: Whether the merchant is configured to use Credit Card Batch.

eft_ach_enabled.description: Whether the merchant is configured to use Eft/Ach.

visa_src_enabled.description: Whether the merchant is configured to use Visa Src.

currency.description: The currency abbreviation for this processor.

live_date.description: The date this merchant went live.

created_date.description: The date this merchant was created.

authorized_date.description: The date this merchant was authorized.

temp_disabled_date.description: The date this merchant was temp disabled.

last_enabled_date.description: The date that an account was enabled after being 'temp disabled'

disabled_date.description: The date this merchant was disabled.

batch_line_limit.description: Processing limit per line for batches.

name.description: Can be either 'Search' for all fields or 'TransHistoryMinimal' for a subset of the fields returned in the results.

start_date.description: The start date (inclusive) '2015-04-22T10:03:19' in the timezone of your merchant account.

end_date.description: The end date (inclusive) '2015-04-22T10:03:19' in the timezone of your merchant account.

end_row.description: Used to page the results. 1-based. This should always be 1 larger than start_row with a maximum difference of 500 between them.

criteria.description: Optional search criteria. All criteria are ANDed together.

field.description: Refer to the [table on this page](https://docs.na.worldline-solutions.com/build-your-integration/payment-apis/payment-sdks/analyze-payments) for the searchable fields

operator.description: URL encoded comparators such as less than, greater than, equals... Refer to the [table on this page](https://docs.na.worldline-solutions.com/build-your-integration/payment-apis/payment-sdks/analyze-payments) for the full list of comparators

value.description: The value you want to match against.

trn_masked_card.description: The credit card with the middle digits redacted with X's

b_phone_type.description: The phone type of the phone number provided - m (Mobile), h (Home), or w (Work).

customer_code.description: The Payment Profile ID used in this transaction, if appropriate.

merchant_id.description: The id of the merchant for this transaction.

merchant_name.description: The name of the merchant for this transaction.

entry_method.description: How the transaction was initiated. N = Standard Entry, A = Apple Pay, D = Apple Pay (External Decryption), P = Google Pay, V = Visa Checkout, T = TD Token

authorizing_merchant_id.description: The id of the merchant that authorized this transaction.

version_3ds.description: The version of 3D Secure being used.  This should return 2 since 1 is no longer supported.  0 is returned if not 3D Secure.

flow_type_3ds.description: The 3D Secure authenication flow type. F = Frictionless, C = Challenge

status_3ds.description: The result returned back from the 3D Secure authenication.

reason_merchant_3ds.description: For merchant use only. Provides a category which identifies the reason for the rejection. [See list of reasons](https://docs.na.worldline-solutions.com/build-your-integration/security-and-fraud-APIs/3ds-20/)

reason_cardholder_3ds.description: The reason for the rejection and instructions on what to do. This can be returned to the cardholder.

downgraded_3ds.description: Indicates whether or not the 3DS transaction was downgraded. A downgraded transaction does not provide a liability shift to the merchant, even if the 3DS authentication result was "Success" or "Attempted".

device_channel.description: Type of channel used to initiate the transaction. 02 = Browser, 03 = 3DS Requestor.

method_url_3ds.description: The outcome of a 3DS Method URL operation performed during the course of a transaction. Either Succeeded (3DS Method URL was initiated and was successful), Failed (3DS Method URL was initiated but failed. The transaction will proceed but without the support of Method URL.)or NotAttempted (3DS Method URL was not requested for this transaction or a validation error existed in the supplied threeDS_server_transaction_id).

message.description: Message containing contextual information about the response

last_transaction.description: date and time of last transaction

modified_date.description: date and time the profile was last modified

card_type.description: The type of card associated with the profile. AM = American Express, DI = Diners, JB = JCB, MC = MasterCard, NN = Discover, VI = Visa

limited_use_card_token.description: Uniquely identifies specific cards in payment profile accounts. Used only for performing Method URL operations.

message.description: Message containing details of the request status

card_id.description: 1-indexed card ID. Total number of cards allowed is configured in your merchant account.

function.description: Will say DEF for the default card

card_type.description: The type of card associated with the profile. AM = American Express, DI = Diners, JB = JCB, MC = MasterCard, NN = Discover, VI = Visa

limited_use_card_token.description: Uniquely identifies specific cards in payment profile accounts. Used only for performing Method URL operations.

customer_code.description: Unique identifier for a Payment Profile. This is an optional field and if not included a randomly generated value will be returned.

comment.description: Any comments that a user would like to attach to the Payment Profile

validate.description: Set to true to automatically process a pre-auth transaction for validation purposes. Processor token transactions require validation, and must be set to true.

comment.description: Any comments that a user would like to attach to the Payment Profile

validate.description: Set to true to automatically process a pre-auth transaction for validation purposes. Processor token transactions require validation, and must be set to true.

cvd.description: Security code on the back of the credit card. This can be set to mandatory in the back office.

account_number.description: For U.S. and Canadian bank accounts. Specify the customer’s bank account number. Account number may vary in length.

bank_account_type.description:
PC=U.S. Personal Checking account.
PS=U.S. Personal Saving account.
CC=U.S. Corporate Checking account.
CS=U.S. Corporate Savings account.
CA=Canadian account.

branch_number.description: For Canadian bank accounts only. Specify the customer’s bank branch number.

institution_number.description: For Canadian bank accounts only. Specify the institution number of the customer’s financial institution.

routing_number.description: For U.S. bank accounts only. Specify the customer’s routing number.

create_from_id.description: The id of the transaction the user wishes to create a profile from.

code.description: The token received from the client-side tokenization service.

customer_code.description: the ID of the payment profile. You need to keep track of this value.

limited_use_card_token.description: Uniquely identifies specific cards in payment profile accounts. Used only for performing Method URL operations.

customer_code.description: the ID of the payment profile. You need to keep track of this value.

state.description: The state of the Recurring Payment account

amount.description: A decimal value in dollars. Uses up to two decimal places.

process_back_payments.description: If a recurring payment is disabled and later reactivated, back payments will be charged if enabled.

card_type.description: The type of card used in the transaction. AM = American Express, DI = Diners, JB = JCB, MC = MasterCard, NN = Discover, VI = Visa, PV = Visa Debit, MD = Debit Mastercard

period.description: The billing period is the regular interval at which credit card transactions are charged

expiry_date.description: The last date of the billing cycle. If never_expires is true, default expiry date is returned i.e. 1900-01-01T00:00:00. Format: 2024-01-01T00:00:00

never_expires.description: Card incurs charges with no end date

bill_at_end_of_month.description: Card will be charged at the end of the month

billing_date.description: Start date of the billing cycle. Format: 2024-01-01T00:00:00

billing_end.description: Last date of the billing cycle. Format: 2024-01-01T00:00:00

amount.description: A decimal value in dollars. Uses up to two decimal places.

total_amount.description: Total amount of transaction in dollars and cents

tax1.description: Total amount of tax1 in dollars and cents

tax2.description: Total amount of tax2 in dollars and cents

reference.description: Not used. Always returns an empty string.

approved.description: Approval status of payment transaction. 0 if the transaction is not approved. 1 if the transaction is approved.

message.description: Message containing information about the transactions status

amount.description: A decimal value in dollars. Uses up to two decimal places.

cvd.description: Security code on the back of the credit card.

token.description: The token representing the credit card number that you will send for purchases. You will always receive a token, even if the request data is wrong or the card is invalid.

code.description: Response code for the message. It can be ignored.

version.description: Message version number. Can be ignored.

message.description: Response message with any hints as to what might have gone wrong, if something went wrong.

CvdResult.description: Result of CVD verification. 1=CVD Match, 2=CVD Mismatch, 3=CVD Not Verified, 4=CVD Should have been present, 5=CVD Issuer unable to process request, 6=CVD Not Provided

CavvResult.description: Result code returned back from performing the 3D Secure Code validation.

customer_code.description:
* Visa - Used by merchant to identify the purchase (i.e. SKU number).
* Master Card - Code the cardholder supplied to the merchant.
* American Express - Customer reference number.

order_tax1_price.description:
* Visa - Amount of state or provincial tax included in the transaction amount - Must between 0.1% and 22% of the transaction amount.
* Master Card - Sales tax/VAT on the total purchase - Must be between 0.1% and 30% of the transaction amount.
* American Express - Transaction tax amount (calculated or entered).

order_tax2_price.description:
* Visa - Amount of state or provincial tax included in the transaction amount - Must between 0.1% and 22% of the transaction amount.
* Master Card - Sales tax/VAT on the total purchase - Must be between 0.1% and 30% of the transaction amount.
* American Express - Transaction tax amount (calculated or entered).

Level2.description: Level 2 processing is for B2B customers. Additional information can be provided, to the benefit of corporate/government/industrial customers, that includes a customer code & tax amounts.

type.description:
* not\_card\_on_file - A transaction where the credit card information is not stored on file.
* first_installment - The first payment in a series of installment payments.  (Eg. Initial installment payment when purchasing a fridge.)
* subsequent_installment - A subsequent payment (ie. not the first) in a series of installment payments.  (Eg. Next installment payment when purchasing a fridge.)
* first_recurring - The first payment in a series of scheduled payments with no expected end date.  (Eg. Initial payment for a subscription service.)
* subsequent_recurring - A subsequent payment (ie. not the first) in a series of scheduled payments with no expected end date.  (Eg. Next payment for a subscription service.)
* subsequent\_customer_initiated - A recurring transaction that is initiated by the customer.  (Eg. Customer reloads a prepaid cash card.)
* subsequent_unscheduled - An unscheduled recurring transaction that is initiated by the merchant. (Eg. Merchant auto-reloads a prepaid cash card for the customer when the balance on the card reaches a predetermined threshold.)

series_id.description: The identifier for the set of transactions that this transaction belongs to.  This field is required for "subsequent\_installment", "subsequent\_recurring", and "subsequent\_unscheduled" credential-on-file transaction types.

CardOnFile.description: When processing a transaction where the credit card information is stored on file, you must pass along an indicator showing the type of credential-on-file transaction that is being processed.

payment_method.description: For a 3D Secure payment process the value for this property will always be 'card'. card_response: $ref:

pa_res.description: For a 3D Secure payment process this stores the Authentication Code (PaRes).  NOTE: Only used for 3DS 1, use cres for 3DS 2.

cres.description: For a 3D Secure payment process this stores the Authentication Code.

enabled.description: If 3D Secure is enabled on the merchant account, set to false to process transactions without 3D Secure.  Otherwise, set to true to process transactions with 3D Secure.

version.description: Version of 3DS to perform (1 or 2).  Optional and will default to what is configured in the merchants account.

auth_required.description: If set to true the transaction will not continue processing unless 3DS authentication is successful.

xid.description: The transaction ID (XID) used for processing transactions with 3D Secure.

cavv.description: The cardholder authentication verification value (CAVV/UCAF/AEVV) used for processing transactions with 3D Secure.

eci.description: The electronic commerce indicator (ECI) used for processing transactions with 3D Secure.

3DSecure.description:
Payment with 3D Secure (Verified by Visa, Mastercard SecureCode, American Express SafeKey).
Note that 3D Secure must be enabled on the merchant account in order to process transactions with 3D Secure.

status.description: Status of 3D secure authentication for the transaction. One of: Succeeded, Attempted, Rejected, Failed, Unavailable, or Error

downgraded.description: Indicates whether or not the 3DS transaction was downgraded. A downgraded transaction does not provide a liability shift to the merchant, even if the 3DS authentication result was "Success" or "Attempted".

reason_merchant.description: For merchant use only. Provides a category which identifies the reason for the rejection. [See list of reasons](https://docs.na.worldline-solutions.com/build-your-integration/security-and-fraud-APIs/3ds-20/)

reason_cardholder.description: The reason for the rejection and instructions on what to do. This can be returned to the cardholder.

method_url.description: The outcome of a 3DS Method URL operation performed during the course of a transaction. Either Succeeded (3DS Method URL was initiated and was successful), Failed (3DS Method URL was initiated but failed. The transaction will proceed but without the support of Method URL.) or NotAttempted (3DS Method URL was not requested for this transaction or a validation error existed in the supplied threeDSservertransaction_id).

reason_merchant.description: For merchant use only. Provides a category which identifies the reason for the rejection. [See list of reasons](https://docs.na.worldline-solutions.com/build-your-integration/security-and-fraud-APIs/3ds-20/)

reason_cardholder.description: The reason for the rejection and instructions on what to do. This can be returned to the cardholder.

downgraded.description: Indicates whether or not the 3DS transaction was downgraded. A downgraded transaction does not provide a liability shift to the merchant, even if the 3DS authentication result was "Success" or "Attempted".

method_url.description: The outcome of a 3DS Method URL operation performed during the course of a transaction. Either Succeeded (3DS Method URL was initiated and was successful), Failed (3DS Method URL was initiated but failed. The transaction will proceed but without the support of Method URL.) or NotAttempted (3DS Method URL was not requested for this transaction or a validation error existed in the supplied threeDSservertransaction_id).

accept_header.description: HTTP Accept Header returned by the browser.

java_enabled.description: True if the browser supports Java.

language.description: Language currently set in the browser.

screen_height.description: Height, in pixels, of the screen.

screen_width.description: Width, in pixels, of the screen.

user_agent.description: User-Agent header returned by the browser in the client HTTP request.

javascript_enabled.description: True if the browser has JavaScript enabled.

BrowserData.description: Data collected about the cardholders browser, required for 3DS 2.0 transactions, optional for others.

type.description:
* not\_card\_on\_file - A transaction where the credit card information is not stored on file.
* first_installment - The first payment in a series of installment payments.  (Eg. Initial installment payment when purchasing a fridge.)
* subsequent_installment - A subsequent payment (ie. not the first) in a series of installment payments.  (Eg. Next installment payment when purchasing a fridge.)
* first_recurring - The first payment in a series of scheduled payments with no expected end date.  (Eg. Initial payment for a subscription service.)
* subsequent_recurring - A subsequent payment (ie. not the first) in a series of scheduled payments with no expected end date.  (Eg. Next payment for a subscription service.)
* subsequent\_customer_initiated - A recurring transaction that is initiated by the customer.  (Eg. Customer reloads a prepaid cash card.)
* subsequent_unscheduled - An unscheduled recurring transaction that is initiated by the merchant. (Eg. Merchant auto-reloads a prepaid cash card for the customer when the balance on the card reaches a predetermined threshold.)

series_id.description: The identifier for the set of transactions that this transaction belongs to.

td_token_status.description:
This field is returned only on response message and it will indicate the status of generating the PT and/or RT tokens. Valid values are:
* 0 = successful – PT and/or RT were generated
* 1 = PT token could not be generated
* 2 = RT token could not be generated
* 3 = PT and RT tokens could not be generated
* 4 = Invalid instance value
* 5 = PT not able to detokenize

SourceData.description: This will contain processor specific data related to the tokenization feature returned for informational use only.

Passcode.description: Pass the authorization header in your request as "Authorization: Passcode Base64Encoded(merchant_id:passcode)"
Passcode.name: Authorization

Sub-Merchant-Id.description: OPTIONAL: Only used by partners to make an API call on behalf of their subMerchant. Pass the header in your request as "Sub-Merchant-Id: sub_merchant_id"
Sub-Merchant-Id.name: Sub-Merchant-Id
